channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: No Rio, o poder político vira um outro ramo do crime organizado
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro resigned to avoid impeachment and was subsequently barred from running for office for eight years by the TSE for abuse of economic power and misuse of public funds. Despite claiming to fight organized crime in his resignation speech, his conviction reinforces the perception that political power in the state has become another branch of organized crime.

item.title: Bolsonaro está na domiciliar pela saúde, não para ser Capitão de campanha
item.description: Jair Bolsonaro has been placed under 90 days of humanitarian house arrest, with strict restrictions including a ban on cell phone, internet, and any form of communication, preventing him from conducting political activities or campaigns. The measure, ordered by Minister Alexandre de Moraes, ensures the enforcement of his sentence, ending the prior use of his residence as an electoral headquarters.

item.title: Calote bilionário de 15 países é socializado entre os brasileiros
item.description: The Brazilian government has submitted to the Senate a map of debt owed by 15 countries that have failed to pay export credit and insurance obligations for nearly two decades, totaling approximately $10 billion (53 billion reais). The largest debt is owed by Venezuela, exceeding $6 billion, stemming from loans to Odebrecht and Andrade Gutierrez during the Lula and Chávez/Maduro administrations, with funds allegedly tied to Venezuelan kleptocracy. Since the debt is unlikely to be recovered, the financial burden is socialized onto Brazilian taxpayers through the National Treasury.

item.title: Saiba o que falta para o reajuste dos servidores de MG começar a valer
item.description: The 5.4% salary adjustment for Minas Gerais public servants still requires second-round voting in the ALMG, gubernatorial sanction, and official publication to take effect. Due to electoral calendar restrictions, the measure must be finalized by April 4 to avoid legal prohibitions on real-term increases in the 180 days before the election. The government aims to conclude voting by Thursday (26), with retroactive payment to January 1, 2026 for executive branch workers and varied percentages for other branches.

item.title: “Sempre governei dentro da legalidade”, diz Castro
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) stated he always acted within the law and plans to appeal the TSE’s ruling declaring him ineligible over alleged use of public machinery to benefit his 2022 campaign. He emphasized that the TRE-RJ had previously found the accusations did not affect the electoral outcome, but the TSE majority disagreed, determining there was misuse of temporary hires for political ends and abuse of political and economic power.

item.title: Campanha de Flávio já fala em levar eleição em primeiro turno
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro’s (PL-RJ) inner campaign circle believes that, following the withdrawal of Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior (PSD), the senator has a real chance to win the 2026 election in the first round. Allies such as Senator Rogério Marinho share this view, citing a favorable political landscape due to opposition fragmentation and the strengthening of the bolsonarist base.

item.title: PSB prepara evento para filiação de Simone Tebet em SP
item.description: The PSB is organizing an event for the affiliation of Minister Simone Tebet in the São Paulo Legislative Assembly on the evening of March 27, with expected attendance from Geraldo Alckmin and João Campos. Tebet is set to run for the Senate in São Paulo in 2026 on Fernando Haddad's (PT) ticket against Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans). The party also advocates for Márcio França as the gubernatorial candidate to strengthen Haddad's slate.

item.title: A desistência de Ratinho Júnior e o histórico instável da ‘terceira via’ no Brasil
item.description: Ratinho Júnior's (PSD) withdrawal from the 2026 presidential race underscores the unstable history of the 'third way' in Brazil, which, despite governing under Fernando Henrique Cardoso, struggles to emerge as a viable alternative to the Lula-Bolsonaro polarization. Past attempts, such as Ciro Gomes in 2002 and Marina Silva in 2009, yielded limited electoral results, revealing that centrist candidacies face significant challenges amid the dominance of the PT and anti-PT forces.

item.title: Pré-candidatura de Caiado divide opiniões na bancada do PSD no Congresso
item.description: The pre-candidacy of Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) for the Brazilian presidency is met with enthusiasm by part of the PSD congressional caucus, but it is not a consensus, as he competes with Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite for the party's nomination in the 2026 election.

item.title: Bolsonaro em prisão domiciliar: como a imprensa internacional repercutiu decisão de Moraes
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes granted temporary house arrest to Jair Bolsonaro following his hospitalization for bronchopneumonia, with a 90-day period for full recovery. The decision was widely covered by international media, particularly El País, which noted pressure from Bolsonaro’s family and allies, as well as his history of violating precautionary measures while under house arrest. Bolsonaro remains banned from using social media, cellphones, or recording audio/video during this period.

item.title: Escândalo do Master é ruim para Lula, mas não muda rumo da eleição, diz fundador de instituto de pesquisa
item.description: The Master Bank scandal is damaging to Lula's image but will not change the outcome of the 2026 presidential election, according to Maurício Moura, founder of the Ideia institute. He states that only 3% to 4% of Lula's 2022 voters remain undecided and will decide the election by a narrow margin. Meanwhile, the Senate race will be shaped by debate over the possible impeachment of STF ministers, fueled by the scandal.

item.title: Que tal Alckmin?
item.description: A poll conducted by Blog do Noblat asked readers whether Geraldo Alckmin should run for president instead of Lula. Of the 2,001 respondents, 38.1% thought it would be a good idea, while 61.9% considered the proposal foolish. The results show significant resistance among the blog's readership to replacing Lula with Alckmin as the presidential candidate.

item.title: Desaprovação de Lula vai a 61%, a maior em 2 anos
item.description: President Lula's disapproval rating reached 61%, the highest in two years, signaling a significant deterioration in public perception of his personal performance. While federal government approval stands at 37%, the gap between supporters and opponents of Lula has tripled from 11 to 30 points since March 2024. The survey, conducted March 21-23, 2026 with 2,500 interviews, presents a challenging outlook for his re-election bid six months before the first round.

item.title: Cai para 32% os que acham governo Lula melhor que o de Bolsonaro
item.description: The PoderData survey conducted from March 21 to 23 shows that 32% of voters consider Lula’s government better than Bolsonaro’s, down 8 percentage points from 40%. Meanwhile, 42% view the current administration as worse, 23% see both as equal, and 3% did not respond. Negative perceptions are strongest in the South (52%), Central-West (50%), and among those earning over 5 minimum wages (48%).

item.title: PoderData: 51% acham trabalho de Lula ruim ou péssimo
item.description: The PoderData poll released in March 2026 shows that 51% of voters view President Lula's performance as 'bad' or 'terrible', up 7 points since January. Approval of his federal administration dropped to 37%, while disapproval rose to 57%, indicating growing dissatisfaction both personally and institutionally. Negative assessments are strongest in the Southeast and South regions, whereas the Northeast and North retain relatively higher approval rates.

item.title: Moraes ignora um dos filhos de Bolsonaro ao autorizar visitas em casa
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes authorized 90 days of house arrest for Jair Bolsonaro, allowing visits only from his sons Flávio, Carlos, and Jair Renan on Wednesdays and Saturdays during specific time slots. Eduardo Bolsonaro was excluded from the visitation list, maintaining a judicial ban on contact with his father, as established in a July 2025 ruling concerning an alleged scheme to pressure the STF.

item.title: Moro se autoconverte em "democrata" entre aspas
item.description: Sergio Moro joined the PL party and claimed Lula was elected 'in quotes,' asserting the president sides with criminals and downplays crime. The event featured Flávio Bolsonaro, implicated in the 'rachadinha' scheme, and Valdemar Costa Neto, convicted in the mensalão scandal. Moro's rhetoric aims to reposition him as a democratic opposition figure, despite aligning with individuals tied to corruption networks.

item.title: Saiba como cada senador votou no projeto da misoginia
item.description: The Senate approved Bill 896/2023, which adds misogyny to the Racism Law, with 67 votes in favor and no opposing votes or abstentions. The bill, authored by Senator Ana Paula Lobato (PDT-MA), defines misogyny as hatred or aversion toward women and equates such crimes to racism, making them unbailable and imprescriptible. All PL senators voted in favor, including presidential pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro, while some PL colleagues were absent from the vote.

item.title: Eduardo critica lei da misoginia, mas Flávio votou a favor
item.description: Eduardo Bolsonaro criticized the proposal equating misogyny with racism, claiming it hijacks the conservative movement for an ideological agenda incompatible with Bolsonarism, despite his brother Flávio Bolsonaro and the entire PL Senate bloc voting in favor. He accused allies of advancing a radical feminism that harms men and called for political mobilization to defend traditional values against what he sees as ideological persecution.

item.title: Governo prepara programa para recuperar áreas dominadas por facções e mais rigor nas cadeias
item.description: The Lula government is preparing a decree to reclaim areas dominated by criminal factions and toughen prison rules, framed under the program 'Brazil against Organized Crime'. The measure, dependent on state adherence, includes six action axes such as arms trafficking combat, financial strangling of crime, and prison security reinforcement, and will be published next week with complementary ordinances. The initiative reflects the PT’s strategy to reclaim public security as a central electoral theme in 2026, responding to the far-right’s dominance on this issue.

item.title: Direita quer derrubar projeto sobre misoginia na Câmara
item.description: Right-wing deputies are mobilizing to block Bill 896/2023, which equates misogyny with racism under Brazilian law, making it non-bailable and imprescriptible. Despite Senator Flávio Bolsonaro’s support in the Senate, deputies such as Bia Kicis, Nikolas Ferreira, Mario Frias, and Júlia Zanatta condemn the bill as divisive, ideologically driven, and a threat to the presumption of innocence, claiming it criminalizes men simply for being men and serves as a tool of left-wing oppression.

item.title: Tarcísio planeja "DHPP da Mulher" para combater alta de feminicídios
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas announced plans to create a new specialized unit within São Paulo's Civil Police, dubbed the 'DHPP da Mulher', to address a record surge in femicides, which reached 266 cases in 2025 — an 8% increase from 2024. The initiative aims to strengthen the state's response to gender-based violence and increase women's representation in the Civil Police Council, following national outrage over high-profile cases such as the killing of military police officer Gisele Alves Santana by her husband.

item.title: Jordan e "Brasa": uniforme do Brasil acumula polêmicas antes da Copa
item.description: The proposed red uniform for Brazil's 2026 World Cup kit, inspired by the 'ember' (brasa) and allegedly linked to an unannounced Jordan (Nike) partnership, sparked controversy due to political associations and rumors of government interference ahead of the 2026 elections. Following backlash, the CBF confirmed the official kit will remain the traditional yellow with blue shorts, rejecting the red version. The yellow jersey, previously tied to Bolsonarism, has regained neutral symbolism, while red remains associated with the left in popular perception.

item.title: Em meio à alta do preço dos combustíveis, Boulos recebe caminhoneiros no Planalto
item.description: Minister Guilherme Boulos will meet with trucker representatives at the Planalto Palace to discuss demands such as fuel prices, freight rates, and working conditions, amid pressure from the sector for cost-reducing measures and income predictability. The meeting occurs as the Lula government attempts to curb diesel price increases through a new subsidy of R$1.20 per liter for imported diesel, split between federal and state governments, after prior measures failed to lower pump prices, which are also pressured by the Middle East war.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro não reverterá cenário no Nordeste, diz aliado de Lula
item.description: Minister Wellington Dias stated that Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) will not reverse Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva's (PT) historical advantage in the Northeast in the 2026 elections. He argued that the senator lacks a clear government project and his growth stems from consolidation within the right-wing camp, not real electoral expansion. The Northeast remains a decisive pillar for Lula, with an expected advantage of 9 to 11 million votes.

item.title: Domiciliar de Bolsonaro pode tirar Flávio da disputa presidencial?
item.description: Allies of Tarcísio de Freitas and Michelle Bolsonaro hope that Jair Bolsonaro's house arrest will lead him to abandon his support for Flávio Bolsonaro's 2026 presidential bid, believing Michelle could persuade him to back her or the governor instead. However, PL and Centrão leaders consider this unlikely, as Flávio's candidacy is already consolidated and house arrest may increase Bolsonaro's influence over his son's pre-campaign.

item.title: A corrida pela Presidência: Ratinho Junior fora e a terceira via - O Assunto #1687
item.description: Ratinho Junior withdrew from the PSD's presidential pre-candidacy, strengthening Ronaldo Caiado's position as the party's leading contender. Eduardo Leite reaffirmed his intention to run, while Romeu Zema of Novo resigned as governor of Minas Gerais to enter the presidential race, positioning himself as a 'third way' alternative to Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro.

item.title: Wellington Fagundes lidera corrida para governo de MT, diz pesquisa
item.description: Senator Wellington Fagundes (PL) leads voting intentions for the governorship of Mato Grosso in all eight scenarios tested by Real Time Big Data, released on March 24, 2026. The survey interviewed 1,600 voters from March 21–23, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence interval. Registered with the TSE under protocol MT-06241/2026, the study was self-funded at a cost of R$64,000.

item.title: Mauro Mendes e Janaína Riva lideram pesquisa para o Senado em MT
item.description: Governor Mauro Mendes (União Brasil) and state deputy Janaína Riva (MDB) lead the three electoral simulations for the Senate in Mato Grosso, according to a Real Time Big Data poll released on March 24, 2026. The survey interviewed 1,600 voters between March 21 and 23, with a 2-point margin of error and 95% confidence level.

item.title: TSE torna inelegível ex-governador do Rio Cláudio Castro
item.description: The TSE declared former Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) ineligible for eight years due to abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, overturning a TRE-RJ ruling by a 5-2 vote. The ruling was based on the misuse of Ceperj and UERJ resources for electoral benefit, favoring investigated candidates.

item.title: Eleições 2026 terão número recorde de mulheres como presidentes de TREs
item.description: Nearly a quarter of the 27 presidencies of Brazil's Regional Electoral Courts (TREs) are held by women, a historic record in regional election leadership. TSE President Minister Cármen Lúcia is leading preparations for the 2026 elections, scheduled for October 4, with over 155 million eligible voters. On Tuesday morning, presidents of 25 TREs met with the minister to coordinate efforts and ensure peaceful elections.

item.title: Lula quer mudanças no crédito rotativo diante da alta do endividamento da população
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva seeks to lower the cost of credit card revolving debt amid rising household indebtedness that is harming his popularity in an election year. The move aims to mitigate the negative impact of domestic debt on public perception and government support.

item.title: Acordo entre Goiás e EUA prevê exclusividade e sigilo no fornecimento de dados de minerais críticos
item.description: The agreement between Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado and U.S. Charge d'Affaires Gabriel Escobar establishes exclusivity and confidentiality in the supply of critical minerals data, granting the U.S. preferential access to these strategic resources.

item.title: Deputado larga política para assumir a CNN Brasil
item.description: State deputy João Vítor Xavier (Cidadania), after four consecutive terms in the Minas Gerais Legislative Assembly, announced he will not seek re-election in 2026. He will return full-time to the communications field, leveraging over 25 years of experience as a radio host and journalist, to join CNN Brasil. The decision marks a shift from politics to journalism, concluding his legislative career.

item.title: TSE condena Cláudio Castro por abuso de poder econômico e político e torna ex-governador inelegível
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) condemned former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro for abuse of economic and political power, rendering him ineligible for office for eight years. The ruling was decided by a majority, with relator Minister Isabel Gallotti and four other ministers voting in favor, while Nunes Marques and André Mendonça dissented.

item.title: Após condenação de Castro, delegado e ex-secretário do Rio de Janeiro deve ser escolhido como candidato ao Senado pelo PL
item.description: Delegado Felipe Curi is set to be chosen as the PL's Senate candidate for Rio de Janeiro after the TSE declared former governor Cláudio Castro ineligible for eight years. Curi, former Civil Police Secretary and a controversial figure due to Operation Contenção and the arrest of councilman Salvino Oliveira, is shifting from a federal deputy bid to the Senate race. His nomination reflects the PL's strategy to retain political influence in Rio despite the legal setback to its original pre-candidate.

item.title: A reação de Cláudio Castro após o TSE determinar sua inelegibilidade
item.description: Former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) criticized the Superior Electoral Court's (TSE) decision declaring him ineligible for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. He stated the decision contradicts the will of nearly 5 million Fluminense voters who elected him and pledged to appeal to the highest instance to achieve what he considers a 'just outcome'.

item.title: Por 5 a 2, TSE torna Cláudio Castro inelegível até 2030
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) voted 5-2 to convict former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro (PL) of abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, declaring him ineligible until 2030. Rodrigo Bacellar (União-RJ) and Gabriel Rodrigues Lopes, former head of Ceperj, were also convicted for using Ceperf and UERJ to create over 27,000 irregular commissioned positions to employ electoral cabos and boost Castro’s re-election. The ruling mandates new gubernatorial elections, vote recount for state deputy, and imposes fines on the convicted.

item.title: TSE torna ex-governador Cláudio Castro inelegível
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) ruled that former Rio de Janeiro governor Cláudio Castro is ineligible due to abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections, with a 5-2 vote. The decision bars him from running for public office for eight years.

item.title: Flávio chama prisão domiciliar de “exótica” e critica prazo de 90 dias
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro criticized Minister Alexandre de Moraes' decision to grant 90 days of humanitarian house arrest to former President Jair Bolsonaro, calling it 'exotic' and contradictory due to its temporary nature. He argued that the measure makes no sense if Bolsonaro's health improves, as he would have to return to worse conditions, and stressed the need for permanent medical assistance at home to prevent risks like falls and bronchoaspiration. Flávio also stated that moving to home is beneficial for Bolsonaro's mental health after describing prison conditions as inadequate.

item.title: O que disseram os dois únicos ministros que votaram a favor de Castro no TSE
item.description: Two TSE ministers, Kassio Nunes Marques and André Mendonça, voted in favor of acquitting Cláudio Castro in the trial over alleged illegal hiring of 27,6 thousand temporary workers in 2022. Nunes Marques rejected the action due to lack of proven electoral impact, while Mendonça acknowledged irregularities but found no evidence of Castro’s direct involvement. The 5-2 vote upheld the conviction but narrowed the margin of defeat.

item.title: Valdemar humilha Moro
item.description: Goiás governor Ronaldo Caiado announced he will resign to run for president as the PSD candidate. Meanwhile, convicted former deputy Valdemar Costa Neto revealed that his new party affiliate, Senator Sergio Moro, helped him avoid being summoned by the Organized Crime CPI, which was seen as a political humiliation of Moro.

item.title: Aprovada adequação da LDO a nova licença-paternidade e apoio à reciclagem
item.description: The Senate approved PLP 77/2026, which adjusts the Budget Guidelines Law (LDO) to accommodate the new paternity leave and fiscal incentives for recycling, with 60 votes in favor and 1 against. The measure ensures these changes are not considered an expansion of tax expenditure, with paternity leave gradually increasing from 5 to 20 days between 2027 and 2029. The bill now proceeds to the Chamber of Deputies for analysis.

item.title: Flávio lidera em MT com 16 pontos à frente de Lula, diz pesquisa
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll shows Flávio Bolsonaro leading by 15 to 16 points over Lula in three first-round presidential election scenarios in Mato Grosso. In the spontaneous scenario, Lula has 20% and Flávio 19%, with 45% undecided or refusing to answer. The PSD ranks third in all scenarios, with Ronaldo Caiado, Eduardo Leite, and the withdrawn Ratinho Junior as potential candidates.

item.title: Farra da PM: Tarcísio assina o projeto que aumenta os chefes sem acrescentar único soldado ou cabo
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas sent a bill to São Paulo’s Legislative Assembly increasing the number of Military Police colonels from 64 to 74 without adding soldiers or cabos, pulling officers from street patrols to serve as aides to senior officials. Although reduced from an initial proposal of 30 new colonels to just 10, the measure worsens the force’s historically low staffing levels, includes broad promotions and increases in other officer ranks, and uses hierarchical ranks as a salary policy tool in an election year.

item.title: Gayer anuncia ato em Goiás e lança pré-candidatura ao Senado
item.description: Federal Deputy Gustavo Gayer (PL-GO) announced a political rally on April 11 in Goiânia to launch his 2026 Senate pre-candidacy, titled 'Wake Up, Goiás! Wake Up, Brazil'. The event will feature Michelle Bolsonaro and fellow PL lawmakers Nikolas Ferreira, Bia Kicis, Carlos Jordy, and André Fernandes, aiming to mobilize the right against the Lula government, the Supreme Federal Court, and to push for amnesty for those investigated in the January 8 attacks.

item.title: Governo lança programa para reduzir tempo e custo logístico
item.description: The Brazilian government launched the Agenda Conectar, an interministerial program led by the Ministries of Ports and Airports and of Development, Industry, Trade and Services, aimed at reducing the time and cost of logistics in national exports. The initiative focuses on digitizing customs procedures, integrating systems among regulatory agencies and the private sector, and improving access to port and airport terminals. Minister Silvio Costa Filho (Republicanos), who will leave office to run for re-election as a federal deputy in 2026, emphasized that the program goes beyond infrastructure, seeking to connect diverse interests to build convergences in favor of Brazil’s competitiveness.

item.title: Fernanda Machiavelli é nova ministra do Desenvolvimento Agrário
item.description: Fernanda Machiavelli, current executive secretary of the Ministry of Agrarian Development, will assume the ministerial post following the departure of Minister Paulo Teixeira, who is leaving to run for federal deputy in October. The announcement was made by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva during the 3rd National Conference on Sustainable and Solidary Rural Development in Brasília. Machiavelli, a career civil servant with a background in social sciences from USP and MDA experience since 2023, is expected to serve for the next nine months of the presidential term.

item.title: Não vamos deixar que potências usem minerais do Brasil, diz Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that Brazil will not allow foreign powers to exploit its critical minerals again, accusing them of repeating historical patterns of extracting gold, silver, diamonds, enslaving Black people for 330 years, and committing violence against Indigenous peoples. He announced the creation of a special council to safeguard national sovereignty over critical minerals and rare earths, established in 2025 to advise on strategic decisions. The remarks were made at the opening of the 3rd CNDRSS, where the government signed 9 decrees for rural development, 7 for agrarian reform, and granted 17 land titles to quilombola territories.

item.title: TSE forma maioria pela inelegibilidade de Cláudio Castro
item.description: The Superior Electoral Court (TSE) formed a majority to declare the inelegibility of former Rio de Janeiro Governor Cláudio Castro (PL) and state deputy Rodrigo Bacellar (União Brasil) for abuse of political and economic power in the 2022 elections. The ruling is based on an alleged scheme of irregular hirings through Ceperj and UERJ, involving cash withdrawals totaling R$248 million, as per the Rio MP investigation. Castro resigned as governor one day before the ruling to avoid immediate loss of office and allow indirect succession via the state legislature.
